skill.name: skill-rails-upgrade
skill.description: Analyze Rails apps and provide upgrade assessments
skill.summary: Analyze Rails apps and provide upgrade assessments
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All static findings are false positives. The 'external_commands' detections are shell command examples in documentation (not code execution). The 'network' detections are legitimate Rails documentation URLs. The 'weak cryptographic algorithm' detections are false positives - YAML frontmatter and plain text were incorrectly flagged. This is a legitimate Rails upgrade assistant skill with no security concerns.

medium_findings.title: Shell Command Examples in Documentation
medium_findings.description: The skill contains 73 examples of shell commands (gh, git, npm, rails) as documentation. These are instructions for the AI to run during upgrade analysis, not actual code execution vulnerabilities.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: These are markdown code blocks with example commands, not executable code. The skill provides documentation for legitimate Rails upgrade tooling.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Documentation URLs
low_findings.description: The skill references official Rails documentation URLs (guides.rubyonrails.org, railsdiff.org, github.com). These are legitimate, publicly-accessible resources.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Hardcoded URLs point to Rails official documentation - no sensitive data transmission or malicious destinations.

low_findings.title: File System Checks for Configuration
low_findings.description: The skill includes file existence checks (ls, cat) for standard Rails configuration files like package.json and importmap.rb.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Standard file system operations for detecting configuration files - no sensitive file access.

dangerous_patterns.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Detection
dangerous_patterns.description: Static scanner incorrectly flagged YAML frontmatter and plain text as 'weak cryptographic algorithm'. This is a false positive - no cryptographic code exists in this file.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: YAML delimiters '---' and plain text descriptions were incorrectly flagged. No cryptographic algorithms present.

content.value_statement: This skill analyzes your Rails application and provides a comprehensive upgrade assessment with version-specific guidance and a selective file merge plan to safely upgrade to newer Rails versions.
actual_capabilities: Detect current Rails version from Gemfile and Gemfile.lock
Fetch latest Rails version from GitHub API
Classify upgrade type (patch, minor, or major)
Fetch and summarize official Rails upgrade guides
Generate file change diffs from railsdiff.org
Create selective merge plans that preserve local customizations
limitations: Does not execute the upgrade - provides guidance only
Cannot automatically resolve all breaking changes
Requires user confirmation before making file changes
Does not test the application after upgrade

use_cases.title: Planning a Rails 7 to 8 upgrade
use_cases.description: Get a comprehensive assessment of what changes are needed to upgrade from Rails 7.x to Rails 8.x, including breaking changes and required file updates.
use_cases.target_user: Rails developers planning major version upgrades

use_cases.title: Safe incremental upgrades
use_cases.description: Follow a step-by-step process that checks for local customizations before updating files, ensuring no project-specific changes are lost.

use_cases.title: JavaScript dependency alignment
use_cases.description: Check and recommend updates for Rails-related JavaScript packages (Turbo, Stimulus, Action Cable) to match the target Rails version.
use_cases.target_user: Full-stack Rails developers with JavaScript dependencies

prompt_templates.title: Basic Rails upgrade check
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the rails-upgrade skill to analyze this Rails application and tell me what version it is running and what the latest Rails version is.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick version check before planning an upgrade

prompt_templates.title: Full upgrade assessment
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the rails-upgrade skill to perform a complete upgrade assessment. I want to upgrade from my current version to the latest Rails. Provide a summary of breaking changes, new files needed, and a plan for handling my local customizations.
prompt_templates.scenario: Comprehensive upgrade planning with detailed analysis

prompt_templates.title: Selective file merge plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the rails-upgrade skill to generate a selective file update plan. Check which config files have local customizations and show me exactly what would change for each file.
prompt_templates.scenario: Creating a safe upgrade plan with manual merge guidance

prompt_templates.title: JavaScript dependency audit
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the rails-upgrade skill to check my JavaScript dependencies. I use npm/yarn and want to ensure my Rails-related packages (Turbo, Stimulus) are compatible with the target Rails version.
prompt_templates.scenario: Checking JS package compatibility before upgrade

output_examples.input: Analyze my Rails app for upgrading to the latest version
What files will change in my Rails upgrade?

best_practices: Always run the upgrade analysis on a clean git branch before making changes
Review deprecation warnings in your current version before upgrading
Test each framework default enablement one at a time
Keep your JavaScript dependencies aligned with your Rails version
anti_patterns: Running rails app:update directly without checking for local customizations
Skipping the JavaScript dependency audit when using Turbo or Stimulus
Ignoring deprecation warnings before starting the upgrade
Enabling all new framework defaults at once without testing

faq.question: What versions of Rails can this skill help upgrade?
faq.answer: This skill can help with upgrades from Rails 5.0 through the latest Rails 8.x. It works for patch, minor, and major version upgrades.

faq.question: Does this skill automatically update my application?
faq.answer: No. The skill analyzes your application and creates an upgrade plan. It shows you what will change and requires your confirmation before modifying any files.

faq.question: Will I lose my local customizations?
faq.answer: No. The skill specifically checks for local customizations in config files and bin scripts. It never overwrites customized files without showing you what would change first.

faq.question: What if I do not have the gh CLI installed?
faq.answer: The skill will still work but you will need to manually check for the latest Rails version. The gh CLI is only used for fetching the most recent release information.

faq.question: Can this skill help with JavaScript dependencies?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill checks npm/yarn packages and importmap pins for Rails-related JavaScript libraries like Turbo, Stimulus, Action Cable, and Active Storage.

faq.question: What happens after the file updates?
faq.answer: After updating files, the skill guides you through running migrations, testing the application, and enabling new framework defaults one by one.
